brainwave_states.description: The deepest known brainwave state. Epsilon waves are extremely rare and associated with the deepest transcendental meditation states and near-death experiences. Some researchers report out-of-body experiences and access to universal consciousness.
brainwave_states.effect: Ultra-deep altered state, possible access to collective unconscious, profound stillness
brainwave_states.subjective_experience: Complete dissolution of ego boundaries, profound bliss or void
brainwave_states.eeg_characteristics: Extremely low amplitude, rarely measurable; some overlap with DC-coupled EEG shifts
brainwave_states.discovery_notes: Not yet fully established in mainstream neuroscience; reported in advanced meditators

brainwave_states.description: Slowest measurable brainwave. Dominates dreamless deep sleep (NREM stages 3-4). Associated with physical regeneration, immune function, and healing. Also present during unconscious or very deep meditation.
brainwave_states.effect: Deep dreamless sleep, physical restoration, unconscious processing, enhanced immune function
brainwave_states.subjective_experience: No conscious awareness (in sleep); profound calm and surrender (in deep meditation)
brainwave_states.eeg_characteristics: High amplitude (75-200 µV), 0.5-4 Hz, global synchrony

brainwave_states.description: Twilight state between sleep and wakefulness. Associated with REM sleep, daydreaming, hypnagogic imagery, and deep meditation. The 'creative' brainwave — many artists and innovators report Theta as the source of their best ideas.
brainwave_states.effect: Creativity, insight, emotional integration, hypnagogic imagery, enhanced intuition, memory consolidation
brainwave_states.subjective_experience: Vivid imagery, free association of ideas, dream-like states, reduced critical mind
brainwave_states.eeg_characteristics: 4-8 Hz, 20-100 µV, dominant in hippocampus and frontal cortex
brainwave_states.notable_theta_research: Hippocampal theta (4-8 Hz) is fundamental to spatial navigation and memory encoding (O'Keefe & Dostrovsky, 1971; Nobel Prize 2014)

brainwave_states.description: Relaxed, non-aroused wakefulness with closed eyes. Berger's 'alpha rhythm' (first human EEG, 1929). The brain's resting-state idle rhythm — present when awake but not actively processing. Associated with calm focus, stress reduction, and the bridge between conscious and subconscious.
brainwave_states.effect: Relaxed alertness, stress reduction, accelerated learning, meditative state, positive mood
brainwave_states.subjective_experience: Calm, present, unfocused relaxation; the feeling after meditation or yoga
brainwave_states.eeg_characteristics: 8-14 Hz, 20-120 µV, strongest in occipital and parietal lobes; alpha blocks with eye opening (Berger effect)
brainwave_states.alpha_peak_note: Individual alpha peak frequency (IAF) typically 9-11 Hz; higher IAF correlates with better cognitive performance

brainwave_states.description: Lower alpha band. Associated with passive relaxation, idle mind, and inward-focused attention. The Schumann resonance first harmonic (7.83 Hz) sits just below this range.
brainwave_states.effect: Passive relaxation, mind wandering, subtle awareness
brainwave_states.subjective_experience: Gentle drifting of thoughts, comfortable stillness

brainwave_states.description: Upper alpha band. Associated with active relaxed alertness and the gateway to focused beta. Often targeted for peak performance and 'flow state' onset.
brainwave_states.effect: Focused calm, readiness for action, creative alertness, flow state entry
brainwave_states.subjective_experience: Clear mind, ready attention without tension

brainwave_states.description: SMR rhythm from sensorimotor cortex, associated with physical stillness and calm focused alertness. Cats produce SMR when alert but motionless. Associated with motor inhibition and focused calm.
brainwave_states.effect: Physical stillness, calm alertness, reduced impulsivity, improved sleep quality
brainwave_states.subjective_experience: Alert but physically relaxed, ready without restlessness

brainwave_states.description: Normal waking consciousness. The dominant rhythm of active, logical thinking, attention, and problem solving. Present during normal alert activities. High beta can indicate anxiety and stress.
brainwave_states.effect: Active thinking, concentration, alertness, logical reasoning, external focus
brainwave_states.subjective_experience: Normal waking awareness, analytical thought, conversation, task focus
brainwave_states.eeg_characteristics: 14-30 Hz, 5-30 µV, widespread cortical distribution; strongest frontally

brainwave_states.description: Highest commonly measured brainwave. Associated with peak cognitive processing, perceptual binding (combining senses into unified experience), insight, and heightened consciousness. Tibetan Buddhist monks in compassion meditation show exceptional gamma synchrony. 40 Hz is the canonical gamma frequency for neural binding.
brainwave_states.effect: Peak cognition, neural binding, heightened sensory processing, expanded awareness, insight, compassion states
brainwave_states.subjective_experience: Exceptional clarity, heightened senses, flow state, profound compassion or insight
brainwave_states.eeg_characteristics: 30-100 Hz, very low amplitude (1-5 µV due to muscle artifact filtering), frontal and parietal lobes
brainwave_states.notable_gamma_research: Lutz et al. (2004) showed Tibetan meditators produce extraordinary gamma coherence (40-80 Hz) during compassion meditation. Tsai lab (MIT) demonstrated 40 Hz light flicker reduces amyloid plaques in Alzheimer's mouse models.

brainwave_states.description: High frequency oscillations above standard gamma. Sharp-wave ripples (100-250 Hz) in hippocampus are critical for memory consolidation during sleep. Also associated with highly localized neural computation.
brainwave_states.effect: Memory consolidation, highly focused neural processing, epileptiform activity detection
brainwave_states.subjective_experience: Difficult to access consciously; reported as extreme clarity in rare states
brainwave_states.eeg_characteristics: 80-150 Hz; hippocampal sharp-wave ripples in sleep

brainwave_states.description: Extremely rare ultra-high frequency brainwave. Only detected in highly advanced meditators or in specific neurological conditions. Possibly related to hyper-efficient neural processing. Sometimes described as the 'polar opposite' of Epsilon waves, with some researchers suggesting a Lambda-Epsilon continuum.
brainwave_states.effect: Rare states of extreme perceptual clarity, hyper-efficient cognition
brainwave_states.subjective_experience: Reported as 'crystal clarity', 'white light awareness', extreme present-moment perception
brainwave_states.eeg_characteristics: 100-200 Hz; extremely rare, difficult to measure cleanly
brainwave_states.discovery_notes: Not yet standardized in neuroscience; primarily reported in esoteric literature and some fringe research

recommended_carriers.use: Delta beats (0.5-4 Hz) — deep sub-bass carrier
